{{Quote|Statement: With all due respect, master, he ''is'' the leader of the Mandalorians. Perhaps my old master was foolish to send me after him.|HK-47 to Revan|<ref name="KotOR game" />}}
An '''unidentified Mandalore''' rose to power among the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] clans following the death of [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] at the hands of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knight]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] at the [[Battle of Malachor V|final battle]] of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. He was the last [[Taung/Legends|Taung]] to hold the title of [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]].

==History==
During the [[Jedi Civil War]], around the time of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] [[Capture of Darth Revan|capture]] in [[3957 BBY]], a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] soldier sent Revan's [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[HK-47]], who he claimed as 'booty' and used to gain rank, to assassinate this Mandalore. However, the Mandalore managed to capture the droid, reprogramming it to kill the usurper, a task at which HK-47 succeeded, though the droid would later consider it his poorest performance.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', Conversations with HK-47 after repairing him</ref> In [[3956 BBY]], he sent a team of Mandalorians to the [[Shadowlands/Legends|Shadowlands]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], and ordered them to test the effectiveness of new [[Cloaking device/Legends|stealth field generator]] technology.<ref name="KotOR game" />

At some time before [[3951 BBY]], he encountered [[Canderous Ordo]], who had recovered [[Mandalore's Mask]] and sought to unify the clans. Already dying, the Taung did not survive this meeting. He proclaimed that Mandalore the Ultimate had usurped the title with Sith assistance, and that he was the true heir of [[Mandalore the Indomitable]]. He asked Canderous to adopt his clan and take his armor, in order to preserve their heritage. Ordo would do this, integrating this Mandalore's mask into his own helmet, subsequently becoming Mandalore the Preserver.<ref name="KotOR CG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Yes, that's him. I toyed with calling him dar'Mand'alor, loosely translating Mandalore the Unknown.|Abel G. Peña confirms the identity of Mandalore|<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/29068832|board=Literature|thread=KOTOR Campaign Guide: Some new info|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2008-08-29|time=3:17 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130523034108/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/kotor-campaign-guide-some-new-info.28966472/page-8}}</ref>}}
[[Abel G. Peña]] has confirmed that this unidentified Mandalore, who is preceded by Mandalore the Ultimate, was intended to be the same Taung warrior that is mentioned in [[Canderous Ordo]]'s biographical entry in the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''. When asked if the two were in fact the same, Peña responded, "''Yes, that's him. I toyed with calling him dar'Mand'alor, loosely translating Mandalore the Unknown.''"<ref name="JCF" />
